rulings.Case Title: IDA CHRISTINA FUA CRUZ VS. JOEL ENRIQUE ANDINO SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT CAROLINE MILLER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. Defendant Miller produces prima facie evidence that she did not own or control the taxi cab that was involved in the subject accident. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 3.) Miller also produces prima facie evidence that she was not in a joint enterprise or partnership with Defendant Sanchez at the time of the accident as to render her vicariously liable for Defendant Sanchez's acts. (UMF 4-8.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Plaintiff. Plaintiff's evidence fails to demonstrate a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant is directly or vicariously liability. Evidence that Miller owned a medallion, and a share of corporate Defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc. at the time of the accident is not dispositive that Defendant was in a joint venture or partnership with Defendant Sanchez. Plaintiff's arguments regarding alter ego and Vehicle Code § 17150 will not be considered as the issues are not in Plaintiff's Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: ABDOL NOVIN, AS TRUSTEE OF THE NOVIN FAMILY TRUST VS. THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Pltff To Provide Further Responses To Csc'S Requests For Admissions (Set No 1) And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, line 1 - DEFENDANT CSC, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Csc'S Requests For Admissions (Set No 1) And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Abdul Novin, has not satisfied his duty to meet and confer in a reasonable and good faith manner. Parties are ordered to meet and confer in advance of the hearing on January 17 in an effort to reach an informal resolution of the issues in this motion. If the parties are able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel for moving party Defendant CSC, Inc., dba Construction Services Company shall notify the court by emailing katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com and all other parties. If the parties are not able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel shall appear and be prepared to discuss the status of meet-and-confer discussions as well as the merits of this motion. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CHAVARRIA et al VS. BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled as to the first, second, third and fourth causes of action. (CCP § 430.10(e).) Each cause of action is sufficiently pled. For the first cause of action, whether Bayview Loan Services ("Bayview") has suffered damages for breach of contract involves a question of fact that is not the proper subject of a demurrer. For the second cause of action, Bayview has pled sufficient facts to allege an actual and present controversy over the insurance policy provisions. For the third cause of action, Great American Assurance Company ("Great American") predicates its demurrer on the failure of the breach of contract claims. For the fourth cause of action, Bayview has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for implied contractual indemnity. (CACI no. 3801; West v. Superior Court (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1625, 1633.) Plaintiffs' dismissal, with prejudice, of Great American is not dispositive to this cross-action. (Johnson v. County of Fresno (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 1087, 1096.) The request for judicial notice of Exhibits A - D are granted as to their filing, but not as to the truth of the matters asserted therein. The request for judicial notice of Exhibit E is granted as the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale is recorded and the facts are not disputed. (Fontenot v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 256, 265.) The additional request for judicial notice of Exhibit A, the insurance policy, is granted as the policy is not reasonably subject to dispute. (Evid. Code § 452(h).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: ERIN MORRISON FLYNN VS. STITCH FIX, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT STITCH FIX, INC., KATRINA LAKE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication as to cause of action 1 for breach of contract is denied. The parties agree that the amendment is ambiguous and there is a conflict in the parole evidence regarding the meaning of the amendment in light of Ms. Lake's explanation of what double trigger meant. The Court overrules defendant's objection contending that plaintiff's deposition contradicts paragraph 8 of plaintiff's declaration. Defendant fails to shift the burden as to causes of action 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, and 10. The motion is granted as to cause of action 6 [failure of consideration]. It is well established that a consideration for a contract is equally valuable whether it move to the other party bound or to a third party. Consideration does not have to move to a promisor." Cechettini v. Consumer Assoc. Ltd. (1968) 260 Cal.App.2d 295, 298. The Court further notes that defendant's separate statement of undisputed facts does not comply with CRC 3.1350 because defendant did not specifically highlight the issues for summary adjudication and which facts support those distinct issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHANNEL PARK DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTs CHANNEL PARK DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A CALIFORNIA, THE RIDING GROUP II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, SIGNATURE PROPERTIES,INC., A CALIFORNIA and DEVCON CONSTRUCTION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER Overruled as to all causes of action. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Liberty Mutual Ins. Co. v. Brookfield Crystal Cove LLC. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 98, does not require Plaintiff to delineate between the defects that causes actual damages and the defects that have not caused actual damage at the pleading stage. Defendants can reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against them. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS SALAS VS. BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTs BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. and BAE SYSTEMS, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Plaintiff's request to continue the hearing on Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication is granted, and the motion is continued to April 4, 2014. (CCP § 437c(h).) Counsel's declaration shows that facts essential to justify opposition may exist, but cannot be presented because of the need to take the depositions of Mark Gasparini, Bill Cahill, Percy Randall Wade, and Antonio Moroles. (Erlich Decl., 3 - 51.) Opposition and reply to the motion may be filed in accordance with CCP § 437c(b)(2) & (4). The trial date of February 18, 2014 is vacated, and the new trial date is set for May 5, 2014. The discovery cut-off date is not affected by the trial continuance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg
